channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NASCAR 25, the new game from iRacing, will put Daytona 500 fans in the driver’s seat
item.description: The Daytona 500 is one of the more challenging races on the NASCAR circuit. The speedway is long and narrow, forcing drivers to be more aggressive. And the weather in central Florida doesn’t always cooperate. During the 2024 event, a deluge of rain had forced a Monday conclusion.
item.dc:creator: Michael Callahan, Fast Company

item.title: The NBA's tech summit turns 25 this year, and its return to the Bay Area is a full-circle moment
item.description: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver had no idea 25 years ago that the Tech Summit would turn into much of anything, much less one of the hottest tickets at All-Star weekend.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin to cut 10 percent of workforce
item.description: Jeff Bezos’s aerospace company Blue Origin plans to cut about 10 percent of its workforce, its CEO told staff Thursday, according to an email obtained by The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Severance is officially Apple TV Plus' biggest hit for years – but it’s still miles behind Netflix’s Squid Game
item.description: Apple's streaming service has a lot to celebrate after the latest Neilson viewing stats, with Severance posting the best numbers since a certain Ted Lasso.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: US lawmakers want Trump to call out UK Apple iCloud encryption backdoor demand
item.description: US officials are urging the Director of National Intelligence to reconsider US-UK cybersecurity arrangements.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Arm secures Meta as first customer in chip push, challenging industry giants
item.description: In a landmark shift, Arm has secured Meta as the first major customer for its internally designed server CPUs, a move that signals its entry into direct chip sales and places it in direct competition with its biggest customers, including Qualcomm and Nvidia.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Prime Video’s Legally Blonde series has found its Elle Woods, and I’m delighted for the comeback of one of the best ‘00s movies
item.description: Prime Video has finally found its young Elle Woods for its Legally Blonde prequel series – I just hope we get a Jennifer Coolidge cameo.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: BBVA permitirá gestionar por primera vez en España cuentas y tarjetas con IA
item.description: Blue, el asistente virtual de BBVA, arranca con hasta 150 consultas y operativas disponibles para clientes, lo que supone dar respuesta a más de 3.000 preguntas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Grupo Piñero optimiza la experiencia del cliente a nivel tecnológico, operativo y participativo
item.description: Grupo Piñero optimiza la experiencia del cliente a nivel tecnológico, operativo y participativo. Se apoya para ello en la plataforma unificada de gestión de la experiencia del cliente de Sprinklr.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Mother of Little Rock homicide victim said son was killed while trying to sell shoes on Facebook Marketplace
item.description: During the last week of January, four people were killed in Little Rock, including a 19-year-old selling shoes on Facebook Marketplace.
item.dc:creator: Gary Burton Jr., NewsNation

item.title: 14 states sue Musk and Trump, calling billionaire's role unconstitutional
item.description: Fourteen states sued Elon Musk, his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) and President Trump on Thursday, arguing that the tech billionaire’s sweeping efforts to cut government spending are unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: AI-powered fitness revolutionizing workouts with adaptive equipment
item.description: “I’m a tech nerd really at heart, and it’s cool to be on that cutting edge,” said Trevor Gere, co-owner of The Smart Fit Method in Northwest Suburban Kildeer.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Barnes, NewsNation

item.title: The Trump administration should follow its own order on free expression
item.description: “If we don’t have Free Speech, then we just don’t have a Free Country. It’s as simple as that.” President Donald Trump said in one of his campaign statements where he previewed how his administration would protect free expression.
item.dc:creator: Kate Ruane, Fast Company

item.title: Those workplace communication tools you hate might actually be good for you
item.description: Many things irk people about the way modern companies operate. Workplace communication tools and so-called enterprise social media platforms are among the low-stakes but high-impact bugbears.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Why asteroid 2024 YR4 is unlikely to hit Earth in 2032 and how scientists keep track
item.description: Scientists say the threat of a newly discovered asteroid has risen slightly in the past few weeks, as the world's telescopes rush to track its course.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: State says $400 million armored EV contract on hold
item.description: The State Department is placing a $400 million contract to build armored electric vehicles (EVs) on hold, a spokesperson told The Hill on Thursday. Tech billionaire Elon Musk’s Tesla appears to have been a likely recipient of the contract, which originated from the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: CISPE raises red alert over Broadcom’s VMware licensing changes
item.description: The European Cloud Competition Observatory (ECCO), a monitoring body established by the association of Cloud Infrastructure Services Providers in Europe (CISPE) as a way to keep cloud licensing practices fair in the region, this week came down hard on Broadcom for its failure to do so.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: House Commerce chair launches data privacy working group
item.description: House Committee on Energy and Commerce Chair Brett Guthrie (R-Ky.) on Wednesday announced the creation of a data privacy working group as policymakers work toward a national privacy standard.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Don’t use public ASP.NET keys (duh), Microsoft warns
item.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence in December observed a “threat actor” using a publicly available ASP.NET machine key to inject malicious code and fetch the Godzilla post-exploitation framework, a “backdoor” web shell used by intruders to execute commands and manipulate files.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Top translation apps for travelers
item.description: Travel abroad worry-free with these top translation apps for travelers. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson highlights some favorites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: VPN vs. ZTNA: Cisco tackles pros and cons
item.description: It’s no secret that more modern approaches to remote access have been usurping VPNs as organizations adapt to the realities of a distributed workforce, cloud-based applications, and heightened security threats.
item.dc:creator: Network World
